changes_made: Changed 'k8s_service_mesh_mtls_enabled' to 'k8s_service_mesh_mutual_tls_enabled' for clarity and consistency
review_notes: The functions generally map well to the ISO27001 A.8.24 control on cryptography. The naming conventions mostly follow the established pattern, but 'k8s_service_mesh_mtls_enabled' was slightly ambiguous. Expanding 'mtls' to 'mutual_tls' improves clarity. All functions are technically accurate and applicable to Kubernetes security features.
title: Use of Cryptography
step1_reasoning: The ISO27001 A.8.24 control focuses on the use of cryptography to protect data. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that Secrets are encrypted at rest, etcd (which stores cluster data) is encrypted, Ingress resources use TLS for secure communication, and service meshes like Istio or Linkerd enforce mutual TLS (mTLS) for secure pod-to-pod communication.
